original: Steam locomotive City of Truro becomes the first steam engine to exceed 100 mph
Chicago White Sox beat Milwaukee Brewers, 7-6, in 25 innings (started 5/8)
Fossil of Chinese feathered baby dinosaur formally identified as Beibeilong sinensis (baby dragon in Chinese)
French adventurer Jean-Jacques Savin (72) successfully crosses the Atlantic Ocean in a barrel arriving in Martinique
New Australian $50 bank note misspells responsibility as "responsibilty" on 46 million notes
Brian Oldfield shot puts 22.86 m ('unofficial' world record, due to his professional status) in El Paso, Texas
Oakland A's Dallas Braden becomes 19th pitcher to throw a perfect game (4-0 vs Tampa Bay)
International Astronomical Union formally adoptsAnnie Jump Cannon's stellar classification system, which, with only minor changes, is still used today
Lisa Henson, American television and film producer (Sid the Science Kid), and CEO of The Jim Henson Company, born in Westchester County, New York
C418 [Daniel Rosenfeld], German ambient musician, and video game and soundtrack composer (Minecraft; Beyond Stranger Things), born in Karl-Marx-Stadt, East Germany (now Chemnitz, Germany)
American Beth Bonner runs female world record marathon (3:01:42) in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Friends of Earth return 1500 non-returnable bottles to Schweppes
Oldest viral DNA, a form of hepatitis B, reported discovered in 7,000 year old skeleton found in Germany
Navajo-American composer Raven Chacon receives Pulitzer Prize in Music for his work "Voiceless Mass"
William Moulton Marston, American psychologist (inventor of an early prototype of the lie detector) and writer (co-creator, Wonder Woman), born in Saugus, Massachusetts (d. 1947)
Eleanor Estes, American children's author and illustrator (Ginger Pye, Moffats), born in New Haven, Connecticut (d. 1988)
Sophie Scholl, German anti-Nazi political activist, active in the non-violent resistance group, the White Rose (Die Weisse Rose), born in Forchtenberg, Germany (d. 1943)
Daniela Silivaș, Romanian gymnast (3 Olympic gold 1988), born in Deva, Romania
Archaeologists hail the Anglo-Saxon tomb found in 2003 in Prittlewell, England, as Britain’s equivalent of Tutankhamun’s tomb as a full report of its contents is published
First newspaper cartoon in America, a divided snake with the words "Join or Die" byBenjamin Franklin, is published in The Pennsylvania Gazette
MLB Baltimore Oriole Jim Gentile hits 2 grand slams vs Minnesota Twins; 4th player in history to hit a grand slam in consecutive innings
The Beatles attend a Bob Dylan concert, at the Royal Albert Hall in London
Barbara Ann Scott, Canadian figure skater (Olympic gold 1948, World champion 1947-48), born in Ottawa, Ontario (d. 2012)
Vance DeVoe Brand, American naval officer, aviator and NASA astronaut (Apollo 18; STS-5, -41-B & -35), born in Longmont, Colorado
Marie-José Pérec, French track and field athlete (Olympic gold 200m, 400m 1996), born in Basse-Terre, Guadeloupe
Luna 5 launched (USSR) 1st attempt to soft land on Moon (fails)
Colonel Thomas Blood attempts to steal Crown Jewels of England and Scotland from the Tower of London, captured running from the tower with the jewels

John Hancockpays duties on 25 pipes of wine, only one fourth of his ship's carrying capacity. British officials accuse him of unloading the rest during the night to avoid paying duties on the entire cargo.
Mihail Kogălniceanu reads, in the Chamber of Deputies, the Declaration of Independence of Romania. This day becomes the Independence Day of Romania.
Piccadilly Circus in London is first lit by electricity
Czechoslovakia liberated from Nazi occupation (National Day)
Laser beam successfully bounced off Moon for 1st time
The Beatles sign their first contract with EMI Parlophone
1st flight over the North Pole claimed byRichard E. Byrdand co-pilot Floyd Bennett. Later discovery of Byrd's diary suggests they may have turned back 150 miles short of the pole due to an oil leak.
Dante Alighieri, Italian poet and author (Dante's Inferno, Divina Commedia), born in Florence, Republic of Florence (d. 1321)
Richard Adams, English author (Watership Down), born in Newbury (d. 2016)
Dorothy Hyman, English athlete (Olympic silver 100m, bronze 200m 1960; bronze 4×100m relay 1964), born in Cudworth, England
Brian Deegan, American freestyle motocross cyclist, born in Omaha, Nebraska
Andrew W.K. [Wilkes-Krier], American rock singer, musician, songwriter ("Party Hard"), motivational speaker, and advice columnist, born in Stanford, California
Andrea Motis, Spanish jazz singer and trumpeter (Emotional Dance), born in Barcelona, Spain
British explorer John Byron, sent to explore the South Atlantic, arrives back in England after circumnavigating the globe as captain of HMS Dolphin
ExplorerAlexander Mackenziesets out on his second expedition to the Pacific from Fort Fork (arrives at Pacific 22nd July)
Isabella "Belle" Boyd, American actress and Confederate spy during US Civil War, born in Martinsburg, Virginia (d. 1900)
Oleg Yuriyevich Atkov, Russian cardiologist and cosmonaut who took part in mission Soyuz T-10, born in Khvorostyanka, Soviet Union
British parliament accepts abolition of slave trade
First ascent of Manaslu, the world's eighth-highest mountain in the Nepalese Himalayas by Japanese climbers Toshio Imanishi and Gyalzen Norbu
George Harrison's "Here Comes the Sun" becomes the first Beatles song to register a billion streams on Spotify; it is also the oldest song to do so
Salem Village Witchcraft Victims' Memorial dedicated in Danvers (formally Salem Village) to mark 300 year anniversary of trials
Gopal Krishna Gokhale, Indian social reformer and leader of Indian Independence Movement, born in Kotluk, Dist. Ratnagiri, Bombay Presidency, British India (d. 1915)
Barbara Woodhouse, Irish-born British dog training expert and TV personality ("walkies"), born in Rathfarnham, Ireland (d. 1988)
Alan Bennett, English playwright and actor (The Madness of King George, The History Boys), born in Armley, Leeds
Nole "Nokie" Edwards, American surf-rock guitarist (The Ventures) and actor (Deadwood), born in Lahoma, Oklahoma (d. 2018)
Sonny Curtis, American guitarist and singer-songwriter (The Crickets - " I Fought The Law"; solo - "Love Is All Around"), born in Meadow, Texas

George Wilson. American basketball center (NCAA C'ship 1962 Uni of Cincinnati; Olympic gold 1964; Cincinnati Royals, Chicago Bulls, Seattle SuperSonics, Phoenix Suns), born in Meridian, Mississippi (d. 2023)
Tom Petersson, American rock bassist (Cheap Trick - "I Want You to Want Me"), born in Rockford, Illinois
Pierre Bouvier, Canadian singer and bass player (Simple Plan - "Perfect"; "Welcome To My Life"), born in Montreal, Quebec
Prince Fielder, American baseball first baseman (6 x MLB All Star, NL HR leader 2007, MLB RBI leader 2009, Milwaukee Brewers), born in Ontario, California
Jenni Hermoso, Spanish football player for Tigres UANL and Spain, born in Madrid, Spain
Beth Mead, English footballer (Arsenal), born in Whitby, England
British inventor Joseph Bramah patents the beer pump handle
